title: Hillary and Tenzing — first ascent of Everest

intro.title: On Top
intro.body: The ninth British Everest expedition placed Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay on the summit at 11:30 am, 29 May 1953. They spent fifteen minutes there. The news reached London on the morning of Queen Elizabeth II's coronation.

stops.name: Kathmandu
stops.note: Expedition arrives March 1953. From here the new southern approach replaces the 1924 Tibetan route.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate, post-war register. Newari brick-and-tile Kathmandu, expedition vehicles loaded with crates, snow Himalayas in the distance.

stops.name: Namche Bazaar
stops.note: Sherpa staging town. The expedition hires porters and acclimatises here in early April.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Stepped Sherpa village on a steep hillside, terraced fields, Ama Dablam pyramid behind.

stops.name: Khumbu Icefall (Base Camp)
stops.note: Base Camp 5,364 m at the foot of the Khumbu Icefall, established 12 April 1953.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Cluster of canvas tents on glacial moraine, jumbled blue ice of the icefall behind, prayer flags strung between rocks.

stops.name: South Col (Camp 8)
stops.note: Camp 8 at 7,900 m, established 21 May 1953. The first summit attempt by Evans and Bourdillon turns back from the South Summit.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Two small tents on a vast windswept saddle of bare ice between two peaks, climber in down jacket and oxygen mask.

stops.name: Summit
stops.note: 11:30 am, 29 May 1953. Hillary photographs Tenzing on the summit holding the UN, Indian, Nepali, and Union flags on an ice-axe.
stops.imagePrompt: Photographic plate. Tenzing on summit cornice silhouetted against sky, ice-axe held aloft with four flags, oxygen mask down on his chest.
